= Customising CC Icons in the Toolkit

=== Expectations
 
This guide will cover customising the colour of your background for your icons, editing the hair colour on the mannequins and similar.

It however does expect that you have a basic knowledge of the toolkits UI.

== Setting up your Project

So first and foremost you want to setup your project, and because of what we are editing I do advise creating a new project. Rather than doing these edits in your primary mod.

The reason for this, is that we are editing vanilla files directly. So in order to cause you less hassle cleaning your mod up before release we instead have a side project that keeps our edits in them.

A pre-made project can be found on this resources page: https://www.nexusmods.com/baldursgate3/mods/132 it already includes all the mannequinns and the background for CC icons for easier access. This tutorial however will still cover finding these yourself.

If you do want the resources from that page you want:
 
 - Genererate less Vanilla CC Icons in the Toolkit
 - Customise Your CC Icons
 
 Both of these are setup as paks but can be easily imported directly into the Toolkit, PAKs were the easiest option for packing as a resource.
 
=== Dependency

While this would be one of the last steps, this is better placed here. If using the generate less cc vanilla icons, I suggest adding it as a dependency to the new project.

Why?

Simply that when you get to the step of creating your icons in your CC projects, it would add both at once rather than having to dig for both.

I do suggest closing the toolkit once and reloading after adding it as a dependency just to make sure all the files have loaded correctly.
 
== Grabbing the Background

We want to go to the Resource Manager and search for `Helper_CC_Background_Vignette_A.` Once found right click and `override in active mod` unless using the base project in which case it is already setup for you in the project. The texture should get added to your projects folders. 

Such as the path below:
`"Generated\Public\customise_cc_icons_9758fff9-a31b-e8b0-1f2d-1ed724cae738\Assets\_Helpers\Helper_CC_Background_Vignette_ABC\Resources\Helper_CC_Background_Vignette_A_BM.DDS"`

You can now edit the texture to create your custom background for your icons, in your desired program. 

Once finished just save over the texture in the project folders. 

Now if you added this project as a dependency to your CC projects or choose to generate the icons directly in this project, they will generate with your newly edited background.
 
== Grabbing the Mannequins

So the bodies you see jumping around the CC icon level, while it is generating your icons? Those are what we want if we want to say customise the hair colour it uses for generating your hair icons.

So first we want to open the UUID Object Editor, and go to `Shared > CharacterCreation > CharacterCreationIconSettings`

This file will help us find which mannequin we want to edit. Though you should never need to edit the RootTemplate, it does help us find the mannequin faster.

```diagram

```


You will see something like this, though the dragonborn entry example on the above table is towards the bottom in the uuid object editor.

- UUID is the unique id for the line.
- Comment is the Race, Body Type and sometimes with the addition of Grey
- RootTemplate is the uuid to the roottemplate used for this icon generation
- Bodyshape
- SlotNames is what tells us which slot the mannequin is used for
- HeadAppearanceUUID is only used for Dragonborn as seen in the last line

As you can see when it comes to assets rather than heads the mannequins are listed as Grey, which corresponds to what we see in character creation for those slots as well.

In this guide we will edit 3 mannequins. One for hair/beads, one for horns and one for heads.

So let's first grab the RootTemplate id of the mannequin we want to edit. 

In this example I will be using Human_Male_Grey so the roottemplate uuid is 8570d6ed-42d9-47a4-886d-c61e125022ca.

image::/customiseicon_roottemplate.png[customiseicon_roottemplate.png, 800]

image::/customiseicon_charactervisual.png[customiseicon_charactervisual.png, 400]

Once searching for the RootTemplate and looking at the information we can see in the CharacterVisual slot it is using `BASE_Humans_Male_Mannequin_8570d6`

Which if we look for in the Resource Manager comes up with `BASE_Humans_Male_Mannequin_8570d6ed-42d9-47a4-886d-c61e125022ca_000`

This is the mannequin we want to edit. Right click and `override in active mod` and then double click to open. We only need to edit the CharacterVisual not the Roottemplate.

You will probably first see:

image::/customiseicon_red.png[customiseicon_red.png]

Do not worry about this warning, this happens because the devs and ourselves are assigning values to the charactervisuals and then removing the assets from the Mannequin that uses those values.

image::/customiseicon_slots.png[customiseicon_slots.png, 300]

On the left hand side you can see we only have Unassigned and Head slots assigned to this mannequin. We want to add hair and optionally a beard too. Click on the + and add Hair (and optionally Beard too)

image::/customiseicon_availableslots.png[customiseicon_availableslots.png]

So we went up with:

image::/customiseicon_hairbeard.png[customiseicon_hairbeard.png, 300]

Now choose a hair/beard you want to view your edits on.

image::/customiseicon_choosehair.png[customiseicon_choosehair.png, 300]

For example the ones I chose:

image::/customiseicon_preview01.png[customiseicon_preview01.png, 200]

Now on the right panel we want to open Presets and go to 03Hair and open that.

image::/customiseicon_presethair.png[customiseicon_presethair.png]

Now you can either choose a premade preset for your icons hair colour here by using the drop down arrow:

image::/customiseicon_choosecolorpreset.png[customiseicon_choosecolorpreset.png, 600]

Or put in some custom values, like say add a highlight. If making custom values make sure to untick Use Preset Values, usually is off by default but if you select a preset and then edit you need to untick it.

image::/customiseicon_customvalues.png[customiseicon_customvalues.png, 600]

This is what it looks like in editor once I have finished editing.

image::/customiseicon_preview02.png[customiseicon_preview02.png, 200] 

Now you have to remember at this step to remove the hair and/or beard before you save. Otherwise you will end up with the beard on your icons. Not the worst thing, as the icon generator will auto change the hair/beard when it comes to generating icons for that slot. But just a heads up that it can happen.

image::/customiseicon_exampleforgotbeard.png[customiseicon_exampleforgotbeard.png]
